meta.title: ghfind | Savage GitHub Score, Self-Test & Discover Top Devs
meta.description: ghfind: a 0-100 GitHub value & trust score plus a savage roast in 30s. See your gaps, self-test and grow, then discover top developers and get found.
meta.ogTitle: ghfind | Savage GitHub Score, Self-Test & Discover Top Devs
meta.ogDescription: ghfind: score any GitHub account and discover top developers. 0-100 value & trust score + a savage roast in 30 seconds, plus a ranked developer board.
meta.twDescription: Score any GitHub dev + discover the best on ghfind. 0-100 score & savage roast in 30s.

loginNudge.title: 🔥 Sign in to unlock more
loginNudge.body: Sign in with GitHub to keep your votes and reactions — and get a smoother experience.

repoLink.label: Source
repoLink.title: Source code & scoring algorithm are open source · View on GitHub

home.headline: Discover the best developers — and become one.
home.subtitle: Start with a brutally honest 0–100 score. See where you stand, learn where to go.
home.disclaimer1: Scores and roasts are generated automatically from public GitHub data only. We roast an account's public behavior and stats, not the person. Results are not factual claims about anyone — please don't use them to harass.
home.disclaimer2: The scoring core is open-sourced as the github-account-value skill · your own API key stays in your browser only.

leaderboard.heading: 🏆 Hall of Fame
leaderboard.subtitle: The Hall of Fame lists accounts scoring 60+, defaulting to Trending.
leaderboard.footerNote: Only high-scoring accounts built from public GitHub data are listed. To remove yourself from the board, request removal here.
leaderboard.loadError: Couldn't load the board right now — try again later.
leaderboard.empty: 🧮 Crunching the numbers — check back soon…
leaderboard.trendTitle: Trend score: 80% score + 15% unique 7-day visitor heat + 5% recent lookup decay
leaderboard.scoreTitle: 0-100 value and trust score based on public GitHub data
leaderboard.trendSubtitle: Trend blends score, unique 7-day visitor heat, and recent lookup recency; heat is normalized so raw attention cannot overwhelm quality.
leaderboard.scoreSubtitle: Ranked by the 0-100 value and trust score.
leaderboard.heatSubtitle: Ranked by total successful roast lookups.

developers.heading: Discover developers
developers.subtitle: Find the top developers worth following, grouped by programming language, project, and organization.
developers.emptyCategories: No categories to show yet — check back soon.
developers.bucketSubtitle: Ranked by overall score, showing up to the top {limit}.
developers.empty: No developers in this category yet.
developers.metaDescription: Browse top GitHub developers by programming language, project, and organization.

detail.backfilling: 🔍 Fetching their open-source footprint (repos / languages / contributions)…
detail.impactSub: Popular open-source repos they've shipped to (commits + PRs)
detail.worksSub: Their own popular and pinned repositories
detail.roastEmpty: This roast hasn't been archived yet — judge again from the home page to generate it.
detail.liveRateLimited: Too many roasts right now — try again in a minute.
detail.liveExpired: The scan expired before the roast finished.
detail.liveGoHome: Roast again from the home page →
detail.battlesSub: PK duels this dev has fought

reactions.heading: Community reactions
reactions.hint: One reaction per GitHub account; switch it any time
reactions.loginRequired: Sign in with GitHub to react. Your choice will be saved.
reactions.failed: Your reaction wasn't saved. Try again later.

detailMeta.title: {username} — {score}/100 · {tier} | ghfind
detailMeta.descWithTags: {tags} — see {username}'s full score report on ghfind.com.

roaster.inputPlaceholder: Enter a GitHub username or profile URL
roaster.byoLink: Use your own model / API key
roaster.scanning: Digging up {username}'s history… reading commits, checking PRs, counting stars, sniffing out farmed activity
roaster.roastThinking: The AI is cooking up a savage roast — coming right up…
roaster.firstJudged: 🥇 You're the first to be judged — a true pioneer
roaster.beatLine: <hl>🏆 Beat {beat}%</hl><muted> of developers ({total} judged so far)</muted>
roaster.privateNote: Note: the score uses public signals only — private contributions don't count, so active members of private orgs may be underrated.
roaster.shareText: My GitHub just got judged: {score}/100 · {tier} ({tierLabel}){beat}. Rate yours 👉 ghfind.com
roaster.errEmpty: Enter a GitHub username or profile URL first.
roaster.errNeedTurnstile: Please complete the verification below first.
roaster.errRoastFailed: Roast generation failed — try again later or use your own key.
roaster.errNetworkRoast: Connection dropped — the roast got cut off.
roaster.errNetworkScan: Network error — couldn't even reach GitHub.
roaster.byoReasonQuota: Free quota's used up 😵 Add your own API key to keep roasting (OpenAI / StepFun / Groq all work).
roaster.byoReasonRate: You've roasted a lot today 🥵 Take a breather, or add your own API key for unlimited roasts.
roaster.byoReasonDefault: No default model is configured on the server — add your own API key to start roasting.

scanErrors.invalid_username: That doesn't look like a GitHub username — double-check it?
scanErrors.turnstile_failed: Verification failed — refresh the page and retry.
scanErrors.rate_limited: Too fast — take a breath and try again in a minute.
scanErrors.github_rate_limited: GitHub's API is maxed out right now — give it a moment.
scanErrors.github_unavailable: GitHub data is temporarily unavailable — try again later.
scanErrors.scan_failed: The scan crashed — try again later.

badge.heading: 📌 Pin to your GitHub profile
badge.blurb: Copy any snippet below into your Profile README. The score stays up to date automatically, and the image links to your detail page.
badge.builderBlurb: Pick a card type, theme, and whether to show a scannable QR — the preview and links update live.

byok.title: Use your own model
byok.compatNote: Works with any OpenAI-compatible API (OpenAI / OpenRouter / Groq / DeepSeek / local). Your key stays in your own browser. It's relayed once through our server to call the model, but never stored, never logged, discarded after use.
byok.tempKeyNote: 🔒 If you're concerned, use a temporary / spend-limited key and revoke it when you're done.

developerCount: <n>{count, number}</n> developers have entered the arena ⚔️

shareCard.brand: 🔥 ghfind · see how you stack up

omnibox.placeholder0: Enter a GitHub handle to start the trial
omnibox.placeholder1: Type a vs b to start a duel
omnibox.placeholder2: Search rust to see who grinds hardest
omnibox.placeholder3: Search a company to size up its talent
omnibox.pkHalfHint: Type an opponent, then hit Enter to fight

vs.metaDescription: {a} vs {b} — a head-to-head GitHub showdown. Who's more hardcore? See it on ghfind.com.
vs.heading: Developer duel
vs.pkSub: Drop two GitHub handles and see who's more hardcore
vs.pkInvalid: Enter two different valid GitHub handles
vs.summonHint: Summon them to the ring to start the fight
vs.bothMissing: Neither has been judged yet — summon them first, then duel.
vs.verdictGenerating: 🤖 The AI is cooking up a savage verdict…
vs.shareText: {a} vs {b} — who's more hardcore? Watch this GitHub duel on ghfind.com ⚔️
vs.trendingSub: The most-watched GitHub developer PKs — tap in for the savage verdict
vs.trendingEmpty: No battles on the board yet. Type "a vs b" on the home page to fire the first shot.
vs.verdictMissing: Hold your verdict — summon them to the ring first.
vs.verdictTie: Neck and neck — nobody would call this one a robbery.

verdictCrush.0: @{winner} steamrolls @{loser} by {gap} points — this isn't a duel, it's a spectator sport.
verdictCrush.1: @{winner} {winnerScore} vs @{loser} {loserScore}: a gap so wide the ref didn't bother with the whistle.
verdictCrush.2: @{loser} is still warming up while @{winner} leads by {gap} — just shake hands and concede.
verdictCrush.3: @{winner} pins @{loser} to the mat; the {gap}-point chasm has no bottom.
verdictCrush.4: No suspense here: @{winner} dominates @{loser} across the board by {gap} points.
verdictCrush.5: @{loser} lost before the expression caught up — @{winner} takes a {gap}-point blowout.
verdictCrush.6: @{winner}'s {winnerScore} makes @{loser}'s {loserScore} look like a sparring partner.
verdictCrush.7: A discouraging margin: @{winner} leads @{loser} by a full {gap} points.

verdictEdge.0: @{winner} narrowly edges @{loser} by {gap} — the win wasn't cheap.
verdictEdge.1: Neck and neck, but @{winner} still laughs last on a {gap}-point edge.
verdictEdge.2: @{winner} {winnerScore} vs @{loser} {loserScore}: victory by a single step.
verdictEdge.3: @{loser} almost flipped it, but came up {gap} points short of @{winner}.
verdictEdge.4: In a tight match, @{winner} pulls slightly ahead and banks {gap} points.
verdictEdge.5: @{winner} wins, but @{loser} made it sweat — just {gap} points apart.
verdictEdge.6: A half-length win: @{winner} clears @{loser} by {gap} points.
verdictEdge.7: @{winner} squeaks through; @{loser} was one kick away.

verdictEven.0: @{winner} and @{loser} are all but even — {gap} points is basically noise.
verdictEven.1: Dead heat: nobody's robbed here — only {gap} points separate them.
verdictEven.2: @{winner} {winnerScore}, @{loser} {loserScore} — a different mood and it flips.
verdictEven.3: Too close to call: @{winner} and @{loser} stand on the same line.
verdictEven.4: Clash of titans — a {gap}-point gap is nothing to write home about.
verdictEven.5: @{winner} leads by a hair, but @{loser} isn't fazed at all.
verdictEven.6: Split the difference — @{winner} just got {gap} points luckier.
verdictEven.7: Six of one, half a dozen of the other — @{winner} and @{loser} gained nothing on each other.
